title: centaurea phrygia subsp. stenolepis
intro: Centaurea phrygia subsp. stenolepis is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant, also known as a type of knapweed, is native to the Mediterranean region and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The leaves and flowers of Centaurea phrygia subsp. stenolepis are used in herbal preparations, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is also thought to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating skin conditions, although more research is needed to confirm these claims.
